# HelpChatbot — RAG-based Blazor Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions from internal product PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │────▶│  Chat Service    │────▶│  LLM (OpenAI)│
│  (Chat Page) │     │  (RAG Pipeline)  │     │  via MEAI    │
└──────────────┘     └───────┬──────────┘     └──────────────┘
                             │
                    ┌────────┴────────┐
                    ▼                 ▼
           ┌──────────────┐  ┌───────────────┐
           │  pgvector    │  │  Postgres     │
           │  (Embeddings)│  │  (Chat History)│
           └──────────────┘  └───────────────┘
                    ▲
           ┌────────┴────────┐
           │  Doc Ingestion  │
           │  (PDF → Chunks  │
           │   → Embeddings) │
           └─────────────────┘
```

## Technology Choices (per skill guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Package |
|---------|--------|---------|
| LLM Abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` |
| LLM Provider | OpenAI | `OpenAI` |
| Vector Store | pgvector via MEVD | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` + `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` |
| PDF Parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig | `UglyToad.PdfPig` |
| ORM / DB | EF Core + Npgsql | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` |
| pgvector support | Npgsql pgvector plugin | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL.NetTopologySuite` or `pgvector` package |
| UI | Blazor Server (interactive) | built-in |

**Why not Microsoft.Agents.AI?** This is a single prompt→response RAG scenario (no tool calling, no multi-step agentic loops), so `IChatClient` via MEAI is sufficient.

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add all NuGet packages (MEAI, OpenAI, EF Core + Npgsql, PdfPig, pgvector)
- Add Blazor Server services and configuration scaffolding
- Add `appsettings.json` configuration sections for AI and Postgres

### 2. Data models & EF Core DbContext
- `DocumentChunk` entity: Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (vector)
- `ChatSession` entity: Id, CreatedAt
- `ChatMessage` entity: Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp
- `AppDbContext` with pgvector extension enabled
- EF Core migration support

### 3. Document ingestion service
- `DocumentIngestionService`: scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Parse PDF text with PdfPig
- Chunk text by paragraphs/sections (~500 tokens per chunk with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into Postgres/pgvector
- Expose as a hosted service or CLI command for on-demand re-ingestion

### 4. RAG retrieval service
- `RetrievalService`: takes a user query, embeds it, searches pgvector for top-K similar chunks
- Apply minimum similarity threshold (0.75)
- Return ranked chunks with source attribution (file name, chunk index)

### 5. Chat service
- `ChatService`: orchestrates the RAG pipeline
- Embed user question → retrieve relevant chunks → build system prompt with context → call LLM
- Persist conversation in Postgres (ChatSession + ChatMessages)
- Stream responses via `IAsyncEnumerable` for low time-to-first-token
- Set temperature=0 for factual answers, pin model version

### 6. Blazor UI
- Add Blazor Server interactive rendering
- `Chat.razor` page: chat message list, input box, send button
- Display streamed LLM responses in real-time
- Show source references (which PDF/chunk contributed)
- Session management (new chat, chat history sidebar)

### 7. Configuration & security
- `appsettings.json` with placeholders (no secrets)
- Document user-secrets setup for API keys
- `IOptions<AiOptions>` and `IOptions<DatabaseOptions>` pattern
- Health checks for Postgres and OpenAI connectivity

### 8. Startup / Program.cs wiring
- Register all services via DI
- Configure EF Core with Npgsql + pgvector
- Register `IChatClient` and `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI
- Add Blazor Server middleware pipeline
- Add health checks

## Notes
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`
- pgvector extension must be enabled in Postgres (`CREATE EXTENSION vector`)
- EF Core migrations will handle schema creation
- Streaming responses improve perceived latency in the chat UI
- Source attribution in RAG responses helps users verify answers

Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions from internal product PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Architecture

│  Blazor UI   │────▶│  Chat Service    │────▶│  LLM (OpenAI)│

- Add Blazor Server services and configuration scaffolding
- Add `appsettings.json` configuration sections for AI and Postgres

- Register `IChatClient` and `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI
- Add Blazor Server middleware pipeline
- Add health checks

- Source attribution in RAG responses helps users verify answers

# HelpChatbot — RAG-based Blazor Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions from internal product PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │────▶│  Chat Service    │────▶│  LLM (OpenAI)│
│  (Chat Page) │     │  (RAG Pipeline)  │     │  via MEAI    │
└──────────────┘     └───────┬──────────┘     └──────────────┘
                             │
                    ┌────────┴────────┐
                    ▼                 ▼
           ┌──────────────┐  ┌───────────────┐
           │  pgvector    │  │  Postgres     │
           │  (Embeddings)│  │  (Chat History)│
           └──────────────┘  └───────────────┘
                    ▲
           ┌────────┴────────┐
           │  Doc Ingestion  │
           │  (PDF → Chunks  │
           │   → Embeddings) │
           └─────────────────┘
```

## Technology Choices (per skill guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Package |
|---------|--------|---------|
| LLM Abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` |
| LLM Provider | OpenAI | `OpenAI` |
| Vector Store | pgvector via MEVD | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` + `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` |
| PDF Parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig | `UglyToad.PdfPig` |
| ORM / DB | EF Core + Npgsql | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` |
| pgvector support | Pgvector Npgsql | `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` |
| UI | Blazor Server (interactive) | built-in |

**Why not Microsoft.Agents.AI?** This is a single prompt→response RAG scenario (no tool calling, no multi-step agentic loops), so `IChatClient` via MEAI is sufficient.

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add all NuGet packages (MEAI, OpenAI, EF Core + Npgsql, PdfPig, pgvector)
- Add Blazor Server services and configuration scaffolding
- Add `appsettings.json` configuration sections for AI and Postgres

### 2. Data models & EF Core DbContext
- `DocumentChunk` entity: Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (vector)
- `ChatSession` entity: Id, CreatedAt
- `ChatMessage` entity: Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp
- `AppDbContext` with pgvector extension enabled
- EF Core migration support

### 3. Document ingestion service
- `DocumentIngestionService`: scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Parse PDF text with PdfPig
- Chunk text by paragraphs/sections (~500 tokens per chunk with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into Postgres/pgvector
- Expose as a hosted service or CLI command for on-demand re-ingestion

### 4. RAG retrieval service
- `RetrievalService`: takes a user query, embeds it, searches pgvector for top-K similar chunks
- Apply minimum similarity threshold (0.75)
- Return ranked chunks with source attribution (file name, chunk index)

### 5. Chat service
- `ChatService`: orchestrates the RAG pipeline
- Embed user question → retrieve relevant chunks → build system prompt with context → call LLM
- Persist conversation in Postgres (ChatSession + ChatMessages)
- Stream responses via `IAsyncEnumerable` for low time-to-first-token
- Set temperature=0 for factual answers, pin model version

### 6. Blazor UI
- Add Blazor Server interactive rendering
- `Chat.razor` page: chat message list, input box, send button
- Display streamed LLM responses in real-time
- Show source references (which PDF/chunk contributed)
- Session management (new chat, chat history sidebar)

### 7. Configuration & security
- `appsettings.json` with placeholders (no secrets)
- Document user-secrets setup for API keys
- `IOptions<AiOptions>` and `IOptions<DatabaseOptions>` pattern
- Health checks for Postgres and OpenAI connectivity

### 8. Startup / Program.cs wiring
- Register all services via DI
- Configure EF Core with Npgsql + pgvector
- Register `IChatClient` and `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI
- Add Blazor Server middleware pipeline
- Add health checks

## Notes
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`
- pgvector extension must be enabled in Postgres (`CREATE EXTENSION vector`)
- EF Core migrations will handle schema creation
- Streaming responses improve perceived latency in the chat UI
- Source attribution in RAG responses helps users verify answers

1. # HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions about internal product documentation (PDFs stored in a local folder), using Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search.
5. 
6. ## Approach — RAG Pipeline
7. This is a **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)** scenario. The user asks a question, the system retrieves relevant chunks from the PDF documentation via vector similarity search, and an LLM synthesizes an answer grounded in those chunks.
8. 
9. ### Technology Selection
10. | Concern | Choice | Rationale |
11. |---------|--------|-----------|
12. | LLM abstraction | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; single prompt→response — no agentic loops needed |
13. | LLM provider | **OpenAI** (via `OpenAI` NuGet) | Widely available; wired into MEAI via `AddChatClient` |
14. | Vector store | **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **pgvector for Npgsql** | Postgres-native vector search; matches the Postgres requirement |
15. | PDF parsing | **PdfPig** (`UglyToad.PdfPig`) | Pure .NET PDF text extraction; no native deps |
16. | Web UI | **Blazor Server (Interactive SSR)** | Real-time chat UX with streaming; no separate API layer |
17. | ORM / data | **EF Core + Npgsql** | Chat history persistence in Postgres |
18. | Ingestion | Background hosted service | Reads PDFs, chunks, embeds, upserts into pgvector |
19. 
20. ### Key Guardrails
21. - Temperature = 0 for factual answers
22. - Relevance threshold on vector search (min similarity score)
23. - Source attribution (doc name + page number)
24. - Secrets via user-secrets / env vars (never hardcoded)
25. - Streaming responses for better UX
26. - Retry logic on LLM calls
27. 
28. ---
29. 
30. ## Todos
31. 
32. ### 1. Project Setup & Dependencies
33. - Convert project to Blazor Server (Interactive SSR)
34. - Add NuGet packages
35. - Configure appsettings.json and user-secrets
36. 
37. ### 2. Data Models & EF Core
38. - ChatSession entity (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
39. - ChatMessage entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Sources, Timestamp)
40. - DocumentChunk record for vector store (Id, Content, SourceFile, PageNumber, Embedding)
41. - AppDbContext with EF Core + Npgsql
42. 
43. ### 3. PDF Ingestion Pipeline
44. - PdfIngestionService — reads PDFs from configurable Docs/ folder
45. - Text extraction via PdfPig (page-by-page)
46. - Semantic chunking (~500 tokens, paragraph boundaries, overlap)
47. - Embedding generation via IEmbeddingGenerator
48. - Upsert chunks into pgvector
49. - Idempotent (re-running replaces chunks for same source)
50. 
51. ### 4. RAG Chat Service
52. - ChatService with IChatClient dependency
53. - Query embedding → vector search (top 5, min 0.75) → grounded prompt → stream response
54. - Source attribution in response
55. - Persist messages to Postgres
56. 
57. ### 5. Blazor UI
58. - MainLayout with sidebar for chat sessions
59. - ChatPage — message list + input box
60. - Streaming LLM responses
61. - Source citations as collapsible references
62. - New chat / history navigation

 # HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan
 
 ## Problem
 Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions about internal product documentation (PDFs stored in a local folder), using Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search.
 
 ## Approach — RAG Pipeline
 This is a **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)** scenario. The user asks a question, the system retrieves relevant chunks from the PDF documentation via vector similarity search, and an LLM synthesizes an answer grounded in those chunks.
 
 ### Technology Selection
 | Concern | Choice | Rationale |
 |---------|--------|-----------|
 | LLM abstraction | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; single prompt→response — no agentic loops needed |
 | LLM provider | **OpenAI** (via `OpenAI` NuGet) | Widely available; wired into MEAI via `AddChatClient` |
 | Vector store | **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **pgvector for Npgsql** | Postgres-native vector search; matches the Postgres requirement |
 | PDF parsing | **PdfPig** (`UglyToad.PdfPig`) | Pure .NET PDF text extraction; no native deps |
 | Web UI | **Blazor Server (Interactive SSR)** | Real-time chat UX with streaming; no separate API layer |
 | ORM / data | **EF Core + Npgsql** | Chat history persistence in Postgres |
 | Ingestion | Background hosted service | Reads PDFs, chunks, embeds, upserts into pgvector |
 
 ### Key Guardrails
 - Temperature = 0 for factual answers
 - Relevance threshold on vector search (min similarity score)
 - Source attribution (doc name + page number)
 - Secrets via user-secrets / env vars (never hardcoded)
 - Streaming responses for better UX
 - Retry logic on LLM calls
 
 ---
 
 ## Todos
 
 ### 1. Project Setup & Dependencies
 - Convert project to Blazor Server (Interactive SSR)
 - Add NuGet packages
 - Configure appsettings.json and user-secrets
 
 ### 2. Data Models & EF Core
 - ChatSession entity (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
 - ChatMessage entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Sources, Timestamp)
 - DocumentChunk record for vector store (Id, Content, SourceFile, PageNumber, Embedding)
 - AppDbContext with EF Core + Npgsql
 
 ### 3. PDF Ingestion Pipeline
 - PdfIngestionService — reads PDFs from configurable Docs/ folder
 - Text extraction via PdfPig (page-by-page)
 - Semantic chunking (~500 tokens, paragraph boundaries, overlap)
 - Embedding generation via IEmbeddingGenerator
 - Upsert chunks into pgvector
 - Idempotent (re-running replaces chunks for same source)
 
 ### 4. RAG Chat Service
 - ChatService with IChatClient dependency
 - Query embedding → vector search (top 5, min 0.75) → grounded prompt → stream response
 - Source attribution in response
 - Persist messages to Postgres
 
 ### 5. Blazor UI
 - MainLayout with sidebar for chat sessions
 - ChatPage — message list + input box
 - Streaming LLM responses
 - Source citations as collapsible references
 - New chat / history navigation
 
 ### 6. DI & Program.cs Wiring
 - Register IChatClient, IEmbeddingGenerator, vector store, DbContext, services
 - Health checks for Postgres and OpenAI
 
 ### 7. Configuration & Secrets
 - appsettings.json — connection string, model IDs, docs path, chunk settings
 - User-secrets for AI:ApiKey
 - IOptions<T> pattern
 
 ---
 
 ## Architecture
 
 ```
 ┌─────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐
 │  Blazor UI  │───▶│ ChatService  │───▶│  IChatClient   │──▶ OpenAI
 │  (SSR)      │    │  (RAG logic) │    │  (MEAI)        │
 └─────────────┘    └──────┬───────┘    └────────────────┘
                           │
                    ┌──────▼───────┐
                    │ Vector Search│──▶ pgvector (Postgres)
                    │ (MEVD)       │
                    └──────────────┘
 
 ┌─────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐
 │  Docs/*.pdf │───▶│  Ingestion   │──▶ pgvector (Postgres)
 │  (folder)   │    │  Service     │
 └─────────────┘    └──────────────┘
 
                    ┌──────────────┐
                    │  EF Core     │──▶ Postgres (chat history)
                    └──────────────┘
 ```
 
 ## Notes
 - No Agent Framework needed — single-turn RAG, not agentic.
 - pgvector extension must be enabled in Postgres.
 - Ingestion is idempotent.
